philosophy: Compounds are meaning-attempts, not canonical answers. Each concept keeps a preferred form and alternate understandable forms. understandability is an advisory ranking aid; human guess-the-meaning playtests decide the preferred form (docs/fonoran-constitution.md).
description: Curated transparent Fonoran compounds with ranked alternates. Preferred forms selected deterministically from ASSOCIATION_SEEDS via fonoran-preferred-select.

preferred.gloss: feeling good inside

preferred.gloss: feeling without motion inside

preferred.gloss: knowing someone is bonded well

compounds.notes: Demoted from primitive — a composite social-emotional concept: knowing someone is reliably good and bonded.

preferred.gloss: body in a bad state

compounds.notes: Demoted from primitive — body + bad is immediate, clear, and campfire-test ready.

preferred.gloss: knowing that goes all the way through

preferred.gloss: staying still through time

compounds.notes: Demoted from primitive — still + time is precise: remaining motionless until something after.

preferred.gloss: the big plant

compounds.notes: Demoted from primitive — a tree is just the big plant; plant + big is universally recoverable.

preferred.gloss: the small person

compounds.notes: Demoted from primitive — person + small is universally intuitive and campfire-recoverable.

preferred.gloss: the outer boundary

compounds.notes: Demoted from primitive — outside + bound says it exactly.

preferred.gloss: the place at the back

compounds.notes: Gap filled — the back + place compound was previously inexpressible without the front/back spatial primitives.

preferred.gloss: the place at the front

compounds.notes: Gap filled — spatial orientation forward.

preferred.gloss: self going to the back outside

preferred.gloss: feeling the need to eat; hunger

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — communicating who needs food first is essential for rationing

preferred.gloss: feeling the need to drink; thirst

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — dehydration kills faster than starvation; must be expressible

preferred.gloss: a protected enclosed place to stay; refuge from elements

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — finding or building shelter is the first survival priority

preferred.gloss: a threat that is close; imminent harm

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — warning a partner of immediate danger could save their life

preferred.gloss: a healing substance or remedy; something that helps the body recover

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — knowing which plants heal is critical survival knowledge

preferred.gloss: to stop and recover; lying still to regain strength

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — exhaustion impairs judgment; rest must be planned and communicated

preferred.gloss: the act of creating fire; starting a fire

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — fire provides warmth, cooking, and protection — making it is day-one survival

preferred.gloss: safe drinkable water; water without contamination

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — distinguishing safe water from bad water prevents illness

preferred.gloss: a harmful substance that damages the body when consumed or contacted

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — identifying poisonous plants or animals prevents fatal mistakes

preferred.gloss: a device or method to catch animals for food

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — setting traps is a passive food-gathering strategy essential in survival

preferred.gloss: food that has not been cooked; unprocessed edible matter

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — distinguishing raw from cooked food matters for safety and digestion

preferred.gloss: food that has been heated and prepared for eating

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — cooking kills pathogens and makes food safer and more digestible

preferred.gloss: comfortable heat; the feeling of being warm enough

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — staying warm prevents hypothermia in cold environments

preferred.gloss: dangerous loss of body heat; hypothermia or freezing

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — recognizing cold exposure in a partner can prevent death

preferred.gloss: blood flowing from a wound; hemorrhage

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — stopping bleeding is an immediate emergency response need

preferred.gloss: material used to bind and protect a wound

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — covering wounds prevents infection in the wild

preferred.gloss: a wound or body part that has become diseased or corrupted inside

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — untreated infections become life-threatening within days

preferred.gloss: extreme tiredness; the body having no energy left

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — recognizing exhaustion prevents dangerous decision-making errors

preferred.gloss: being saved from danger by someone else; outside help arriving

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — the ultimate goal of many survival situations is to be rescued

preferred.gloss: safe to eat; not poisonous or harmful when consumed

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — testing whether something is edible is a daily survival question

preferred.gloss: severe lack of food causing bodily harm; dying of hunger

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — understanding the timeline of starvation helps prioritize food-finding

preferred.gloss: dangerous lack of water in the body

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — dehydration is the fastest non-injury killer in survival scenarios

preferred.gloss: water covering the ground in dangerous amounts; a flood

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — floods destroy camps and cut off paths; must be recognized and named

preferred.gloss: elevated land; a place higher than the surrounding area

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — moving to high ground is a flood and visibility survival strategy

preferred.gloss: a dangerous animal that hunts; a threat from wildlife

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — knowing a predator is near triggers shelter and fire responses

preferred.gloss: concealed from view; not visible to others

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — hiding from predators or threats is a core survival behavior

preferred.gloss: the ability to navigate; finding or knowing a route

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — getting lost is deadly; knowing how to find paths is essential

preferred.gloss: not knowing where you are; unable to find the way

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — being lost is a primary survival emergency that must be communicated

preferred.gloss: the dark gas rising from fire; smoke

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — smoke signals rescuers and also warns of fire danger nearby

preferred.gloss: a fractured or broken bone; a serious skeletal injury

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — a broken bone severely limits mobility and requires immediate care

preferred.gloss: not awake and unresponsive; knocked out or passed out

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — an unconscious partner is a medical emergency requiring immediate action

preferred.gloss: moving while holding something or someone; transporting

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — carrying an injured person or supplies is a constant survival task

preferred.gloss: limiting food or water intake to make supplies last longer

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — rationing extends survival time when resources are scarce

preferred.gloss: a location where clean water can be found; a spring or stream

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — finding a reliable water source is the first camp-siting priority

preferred.gloss: sharing body heat with others; huddling together for warmth

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — in cold survival, sharing warmth between people can prevent hypothermia

preferred.gloss: at the side of; next to

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Telling someone to stand beside you while you work together on a task

preferred.gloss: in the space separating two things

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Describing where to place a fire — between the two rocks for shelter

preferred.gloss: moving in the direction of something

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Signaling to move toward the water source or shelter

preferred.gloss: moving in the direction away from something

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Warning someone to move away from danger

preferred.gloss: to go into something

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Telling your partner to enter the cave for shelter

preferred.gloss: to go out of something

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Telling your partner when to exit the cave in the morning

preferred.gloss: at a position higher than something

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Describing where smoke rises or where a ledge is located

preferred.gloss: at a position lower than something

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Describing where the valley or water pool lies

preferred.gloss: on the other side; directly across from

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Pointing out that food is on the opposite side of the river

preferred.gloss: encircling a place; all around

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Describing the forest surrounding the camp to warn of animals

preferred.gloss: following the length of something; beside and parallel

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Directing someone to walk along the river to find food

preferred.gloss: moving toward a higher place

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Directing someone to climb upward to get a better view

preferred.gloss: moving toward a lower place

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Telling someone to head downward toward the valley

preferred.gloss: moving toward the center or interior

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Guiding someone inward toward the center of the shelter

preferred.gloss: moving away from the center toward the edge

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Spreading out outward to search a wider area for resources

preferred.gloss: a place that is close

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Telling your partner there is water nearby so they don't panic

preferred.gloss: a place that is far away

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Warning that the next water source is a distant place requiring preparation

preferred.gloss: an elevated location; a high spot

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Pointing out a high place to use as a lookout

preferred.gloss: a low-lying location; a depression

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Warning that the low place floods when it rains

preferred.gloss: the outermost boundary; the rim or edge

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Warning not to step near the edge of the cliff

preferred.gloss: a way through something; a narrow route

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Describing a passage through the rocks to reach the other side

preferred.gloss: a point where paths meet or split

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Agreeing on which way to go at a junction so you don't get separated

preferred.gloss: a known reference point used for navigation

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Agreeing on a landmark so you can find each other if separated

preferred.gloss: the way one is facing or heading; a bearing

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Establishing a shared direction so both travel the same way

preferred.gloss: a shorter or faster route

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Telling your partner you found a shortcut to the water source

preferred.gloss: a path that ends with no way through

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Warning that the canyon ahead is a dead end to avoid wasted travel

preferred.gloss: a clearing; an open area with nothing in it

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Pointing out an open space suitable for setting up camp

preferred.gloss: a concealed or sheltered spot

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Describing a hidden place to store supplies or hide from danger

preferred.gloss: directly above; close overhead

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Warning about a branch or rock overhead that could fall

preferred.gloss: directly below one's feet; on the ground beneath

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Warning about unstable ground underfoot near the riverbank

preferred.gloss: the side that is farthest away; the other side

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Pointing out that the fruit trees are on the far side of the hill

preferred.gloss: the interior region of something

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Describing the inner area of the cave as safe and dry

preferred.gloss: the exterior region surrounding something

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Warning that the outer area of the camp is exposed to wind

preferred.gloss: a direct, unbranching route

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Telling your partner to follow the straight path without turning

preferred.gloss: a route that loops back to its start

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Describing a circular path that scouts the area and returns to camp

preferred.gloss: an agreed spot where people come together

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Setting a meeting point in case you get separated while foraging

preferred.gloss: a safe, favorable location

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Identifying safe ground to sleep on away from flooding or predators

preferred.gloss: a hazardous or unsafe location

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Marking dangerous ground near unstable cliffs or predator territory

preferred.gloss: the route back to where one came from

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — Memorizing the return path so you can find camp after exploring

preferred.gloss: the nose; organ of smell on the face

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — pointing to body parts to communicate injury or illness is essential from day one

preferred.gloss: the ear; organ of hearing

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — needed to explain hearing problems, infections, or to point out sounds

preferred.gloss: the foot; base of the leg used for walking

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — a hurt foot stops all travel and foraging — critical to communicate

preferred.gloss: the leg; limb used for movement

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — a broken or injured leg is a survival emergency requiring explanation

preferred.gloss: the arm; limb connecting hand to shoulder

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — needed to describe injuries, carrying capacity, or asking for help lifting

preferred.gloss: the shoulder; upper joint where arm meets torso

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — shoulder injuries affect carrying loads and building shelter

preferred.gloss: the stomach; internal organ that holds and digests food

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — stomach pain or hunger cramps must be communicated for health and rationing

preferred.gloss: a tooth; hard bone-like structure in the mouth for eating

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — tooth pain is debilitating and affects ability to eat — survival relevant

preferred.gloss: the tongue; organ of taste and speech inside the mouth

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — needed to describe mouth injuries or to explain tasting something dangerous

preferred.gloss: the throat; inner passage for speaking, eating, and breathing

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — a sore or blocked throat affects eating and communication — urgent to name

preferred.gloss: a finger; single digit of the hand

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — pointing, counting, and describing hand injuries all require naming fingers

preferred.gloss: the thumb; the large opposable digit of the hand

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — thumb injuries severely limit grip and tool use — important to distinguish

preferred.gloss: the knee; joint in the middle of the leg

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — knee injuries prevent walking and climbing — critical survival information

preferred.gloss: the chest; front of the torso housing heart and lungs

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — chest pain or injury signals serious danger and must be communicated fast

preferred.gloss: the lung; internal organ for breathing air

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — breathing difficulty from lung problems is a medical emergency in the wild

preferred.gloss: blood; the vital fluid flowing through the body

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — visible bleeding from wounds is an immediate survival concern to communicate

preferred.gloss: sweat; salty water released by the body when hot or exerting

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — sweating signals overheating or fever — important for health monitoring

preferred.gloss: a tear; water from the eye when crying or in pain

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — distinguishing pain-tears from emotional ones matters for diagnosing injury

preferred.gloss: a scar; healed mark left on skin after a wound

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — scars help identify people and explain past injuries or dangers

preferred.gloss: tired; the body is drained of energy and needs rest

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — communicating exhaustion prevents dangerous overexertion in survival situations

preferred.gloss: weak; having little physical strength or capability

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — weakness from illness or injury must be communicated to adjust workload

preferred.gloss: numb; unable to feel touch or sensation in a body part

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — numbness from cold or injury signals frostbite or nerve damage — urgent

preferred.gloss: dizzy; a spinning sensation in the head; loss of balance

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — dizziness signals dehydration, head injury, or illness — critical to name

preferred.gloss: blind; unable to see; loss of vision

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — blindness from injury or smoke is an immediate danger requiring communication

preferred.gloss: deaf; unable to hear; loss of hearing

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — deafness affects warning signals and communication strategy in the wild

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — swelling indicates infection or injury severity — important for treatment decisions

preferred.gloss: a bruise; dark discoloration under skin from impact injury

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — bruises indicate internal damage and help assess injury severity

preferred.gloss: an itch; irritating skin sensation that makes one want to scratch

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — itching from plants, insects, or rashes is common and needs to be communicated

preferred.gloss: a rash; irritated or inflamed area of skin

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — rashes from plants or allergies signal danger and need identification

preferred.gloss: the spine; the column of bones running down the back

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — back bone injuries are serious and affect all movement — must be named

preferred.gloss: a fist; the hand closed tightly

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — fists signal fighting intent or gripping strength — useful for communication

preferred.gloss: a fingernail; hard protective covering at the tip of a finger

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — nails are used as tools for scraping and gripping — practical to name

preferred.gloss: the hip; the joint connecting the leg to the torso

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — hip injuries prevent walking and must be distinguished from leg or back pain

preferred.gloss: the wrist; the joint connecting hand to arm

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — wrist injuries from falls are common and affect all hand-based survival tasks

preferred.gloss: a person not known to you; an outsider

preferred.gloss: an older person; one who came before

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — referring to someone with more experience or seniority

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — wariness about a stranger who appeared at camp

preferred.gloss: to follow quickly in pursuit of something

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — chasing an animal for food or fleeing a pursuer

preferred.gloss: to go behind and in the same direction as someone

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — telling a companion to follow your path through unknown terrain

preferred.gloss: to move through or past something

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — passing through dense forest or past a dangerous animal

preferred.gloss: to rotate or change direction

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — directing someone to turn around on a path

preferred.gloss: to cease moving

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — urgent signal to stop before stepping into danger

preferred.gloss: to break and move earth downward

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — digging for roots, water, or to make a fire pit

preferred.gloss: to cause liquid to flow from a container

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — pouring water to clean a wound or fill a vessel

preferred.gloss: to fasten things together with a cord or knot

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — tying branches together to build a shelter or raft

preferred.gloss: to divide something by making a sharp edge through it

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — cutting rope, food, or plant material with a stone tool

preferred.gloss: to cover or encircle something by winding material around it

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — wrapping a wound or wrapping food to preserve it

preferred.gloss: to shut or seal something

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — closing a shelter entrance to keep out cold or animals

preferred.gloss: to move rapidly back and forth or in all directions

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — shaking a container to mix contents or shaking a branch to get fruit

preferred.gloss: to extend a finger or hand toward something to indicate it

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — pointing toward a water source, danger, or direction of travel

preferred.gloss: to hit something with force

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — striking a stone to make sparks for fire or striking an animal

preferred.gloss: to apply steady force against something

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — pressing on a wound to stop bleeding or pressing clay into shape

preferred.gloss: to spread things out in many directions

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — scattering embers to put out a fire or spreading seeds

preferred.gloss: to rest against something at an angle

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — leaning branches against a tree to form a shelter wall

preferred.gloss: to move close to the ground on hands and knees

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — crawling under low branches or sneaking past a dangerous animal

preferred.gloss: to separate something by pulling its parts in opposite directions

compounds.notes: Vocab survey proposal — pulling apart fibrous plants to make rope or separating tangled materials

preferred.gloss: ready

compounds.notes: 'Ready' means prepared/set to act — essentially 'held in place before doing', or 'will + now + hold' (poised to act immediately). The clearest compound is 'hold + before + do' (prepared to act) or 'wi

preferred.gloss: fonoran

compounds.notes: Fonoran as a modifier means 'of/relating to the Fonoran language', which can be expressed as 'language + name' (the named language) or 'shared_meaning + name' (the named shared meaning system). The mo

preferred.gloss: raindrops

compounds.notes: Raindrops are small units/pieces of rain (water falling from the sky), naturally expressible as small particles of rain. 'rain' + 'small' or 'rain' + 'one' captures the individual drop concept well.

preferred.gloss: laughed

compounds.notes: Laughter is a joyful, expressive vocalization — naturally decomposable as 'happy' + 'voice' (a happy vocal expression), which is intuitively recoverable. The past tense 'laughed' is handled by Fonoran
